title: Kenyan Meat Kebabs
description: Juicy and flavorful meat kebabs with a blend of herbal spices and a hint of molasses, cooked to perfection using mushimono steam, a traditional Kenyan cooking method that seals in the flavors and moisture of the meat, resulting in a dish that is both delicious and visually appealing, perfect for special occasions and outdoor gatherings.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together molasses, ginger paste, garlic paste, cumin powder, coriander powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper to make the marinade.
Add the beef cubes to the marinade and mix well to coat.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Thread the marinated beef cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill or grill pan with oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the kebabs for 8-10 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are cooked through.
While the kebabs are cooking, prepare the mushimono steam by boiling water in a large pot.
Once the kebabs are done, place them in a steamer basket over the boiling water and cover with a lid.
Steam the kebabs for an additional 2-3 minutes to lock in the juices and flavors.
Serve the kebabs hot with a squeeze of lemon juice and a side of your choice.
